text.NRSV: The word of the Lord that came to Micah of Moresheth in the days of Kings Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah of Judah, which he saw concerning Samaria and Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: The word of Yahweh which came unto Micah the Morashtite, in the days of Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, kings of Judah,—of which he had vision concerning Samaria, and Jerusalem:—
text.KJV: The word of the Lord that came to Micah the Morasthite in the days of Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah, which he saw concerning Samaria and Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: Chapter 1 The word of the Lord which came to Micah of Moresheth in the days of Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah, which he saw concerning Samaria and Jerusalem.
text.DRB: The word of our Lord that was made to Micheas the Morasthite, in the days of Joathan, Achaz, and Ezechias kings of Juda: which he saw upon Samaria and Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Hear, you peoples, all of you; listen, O earth, and all that is in it; and let the Lord God be a witness against you, the Lord from his holy temple.
text.Rotherham: Hear ye peoples, all of you, Hearken, O earth and the fulness thereof,—and let My Lord Yahweh be among you for a witness, My Lord out of his holy temple.
text.KJV: Hear, all ye people; hearken, O earth, and all that therein is: and let the Lord God be witness against you, the Lord from his holy temple.
text.NABRE: I. Oracles of Punishment Hear, O peoples, all of you, give heed, O earth, and all that is in it! Let the Lord God be witness against you, the Lord from his holy temple!
text.DRB: Hear all ye peoples: & let the earth attend, & the fullness thereof: and let our Lord God be unto you for a witness, our Lord out of his holy temple.

text.NRSV: For lo, the Lord is coming out of his place, and will come down and tread upon the high places of the earth.
text.Rotherham: For lo! Yahweh, coming forth out of his place,—that he may descend, and march along upon the high places of the earth.
text.KJV: For, behold, the Lord cometh forth out of his place, and will come down, and tread upon the high places of the earth.
text.NABRE: For see, the Lord goes out from his place and descending, treads upon the heights of the earth.
text.DRB: Because lo our Lord will go forth out of his place: and he will descend, and will tread upon the highest of the earth.

text.NRSV: Then the mountains will melt under him and the valleys will burst open, like wax near the fire, like waters poured down a steep place.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the mountains be melted beneath him, and, the valleys, be cleft,—as wax before the fire, as waters poured out in a steep place.
text.KJV: And the mountains shall be molten under him, and the valleys shall be cleft, as wax before the fire, and as the waters that are poured down a steep place.
text.NABRE: The mountains melt under him and the valleys split open, Like wax before the fire, like water poured down a slope.
text.DRB: And the mountains shall be consumed under him: and the valleys shall be cloven, as wax at the presence of fire, and as waters that run down headlong.

text.NRSV: All this is for the transgression of Jacob and for the sins of the house of Israel. What is the transgression of Jacob? Is it not Samaria? And what is the high place of Judah? Is it not Jerusalem?
text.Rotherham: For the transgression of Jacob, is all this, and for the sin of the house of Israel,—Whose is the transgression of Jacob? Is it not Samaria’s? And whose is the sin of Judah? Is it not Jerusalem?
text.KJV: For the transgression of Jacob is all this, and for the sins of the house of Israel. What is the transgression of Jacob? is it not Samaria? and what are the high places of Judah? are they not Jerusalem?
text.NABRE: All this is for the crime of Jacob, for the sins of the house of Israel. What is the crime of Jacob? Is it not Samaria? And what is the sin of the house of Judah? Is it not Jerusalem?
text.DRB: For the wickedness of Jacob all this, and for the sins of the house of Israel. What is the wickedness of Jacob? Is it not Samaria? And what the excelses of Juda? Is it not Jerusalem?

text.NRSV: Therefore I will make Samaria a heap in the open country, a place for planting vineyards; I will pour down her stones into the valley, and uncover her foundations.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I make of Samaria a heap in a field, the plantings in a vineyard,—and I will pour down, into the valley, her stones, and, her foundations, will I lay bare;
text.KJV: Therefore I will make Samaria as an heap of the field, and as plantings of a vineyard: and I will pour down the stones thereof into the valley, and I will discover the foundations thereof.
text.NABRE: So I will make Samaria a ruin in the field, a place to plant vineyards; I will throw its stones into the valley, and lay bare its foundations.
text.DRB: And I will lay Samaria as a heap of stones in the field when a vineyard is planted: & I will pluck down her stones into the valley, & will discover her foundations.

text.NRSV: All her images shall be beaten to pieces, all her wages shall be burned with fire, and all her idols I will lay waste; for as the wages of a prostitute she gathered them, and as the wages of a prostitute they shall again be used.
text.Rotherham: And, all her images, shall be beaten in pieces, and, all her rewards for unchastity, shall be burned in the fire, and, all her idols, will I make a desolation,—for, out of the reward of unchastity, she gathered [them], and, unto the reward of unchastity, shall they return.
text.KJV: And all the graven images thereof shall be beaten to pieces, and all the hires thereof shall be burned with the fire, and all the idols thereof will I lay desolate: for she gathered it of the hire of an harlot, and they shall return to the hire of an harlot.
text.NABRE: All its carved figures shall be broken to pieces, all its wages shall be burned in the fire, and all its idols I will destroy. As the wages of a prostitute it gathered them, and to the wages of a prostitute they shall return.
text.DRB: And all her sculptiles shall be cut in sunder, & all her wages shall be burnt with fire, & I will lay all her idols into perdition: because they are gathered together of the wages of an harlot, & unto the hire of an harlot they shall return.

text.NRSV: For this I will lament and wail; I will go barefoot and naked; I will make lamentation like the jackals, and mourning like the ostriches.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, will I lament and howl, I will go stript and bare,—I will make a lamentation, like the wild dogs, and a mourning, like ostriches.
text.KJV: Therefore I will wail and howl, I will go stripped and naked: I will make a wailing like the dragons, and mourning as the owls.
text.NABRE: For this I will lament and wail, go barefoot and naked; I will utter lamentation like the jackals, mourning like the ostriches,
text.DRB: Upon this will I lament, and howl: I will go spoiled, and naked: I will make lamentation as of dragons, & mourning as it were of striches.

text.NRSV: For her wound is incurable. It has come to Judah; it has reached to the gate of my people, to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: For dangerous are her wounds,—for she hath come as far as Judah, she hath reached as far as the gate of my people, as far as Jerusalem.
text.KJV: For her wound is incurable; for it is come unto Judah; he is come unto the gate of my people, even to Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: For her wound is incurable; it has come even to Judah. It has reached to the gate of my people, even to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Because her plague is desperate, because it is come even to Juda, it hath touched the gate of my people even to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Tell it not in Gath, weep not at all; in Beth-le-aphrah roll yourselves in the dust.
text.Rotherham: In Gath, do not tell, in Accho, do not weep,—in Beth-l’aphrah, roll yourselves in dust.
text.KJV: Declare ye it not at Gath, weep ye not at all: in the house of Aphrah roll thyself in the dust.
text.NABRE: Do not announce it in Gath, do not weep at all; In Beth-leaphrah roll in the dust.
text.DRB: In Geth declare it not, weep not with tears: in the house of dust sprinkle yourself with dust.

text.NRSV: Pass on your way, inhabitants of Shaphir, in nakedness and shame; the inhabitants of Zaanan do not come forth; Beth-ezel is wailing and shall take its support from you.
text.Rotherham: Pass thou over (for you), thou inhabitress of Shaphir, of disgraceful disclosure,—the inhabitress of Zaanan, hath not gone forth, at the lamentation of Beth-ezel, shall he take from you his station,
text.KJV: Pass ye away, thou inhabitant of Saphir, having thy shame naked: the inhabitant of Zaanan came not forth in the mourning of Beth–ezel; he shall receive of you his standing.
text.NABRE: Pass by, you who dwell in Shaphir! The inhabitants of Zaanan do not come forth from their city. There is lamentation in Beth-ezel. It will withdraw its support from you.
text.DRB: And pass ye to yourselves o fair habitation, confounded with ignominy: she went not out that dwelleth in the going out: the house adjoining shall receive lamentation for you, which stood to herself.

text.NRSV: For the inhabitants of Maroth wait anxiously for good, for evil has come down from the Lord to the gate of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Though the inhabitress of Maroth waited for blessing,—yet there came down calamity from Yahweh, to the gate of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: For the inhabitant of Maroth waited carefully for good: but evil came down from the Lord unto the gate of Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: The inhabitants of Maroth hope for good, But evil has come down from the Lord to the gate of Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Because she is weakened to good which dwelleth in bitterness: because evil is descended from our Lord into the gate of Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Harness the steeds to the chariots, inhabitants of Lachish; it was the beginning of sin to daughter Zion, for in you were found the transgressions of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Bind the chariot to the steed, O inhabitress of Lachish,—the beginning of sin, was she to the daughter of Zion, for, in thee, have been found the transgressions of Israel.
text.KJV: O thou inhabitant of Lachish, bind the chariot to the swift beast: she is the beginning of the sin to the daughter of Zion: for the transgressions of Israel were found in thee.
text.NABRE: Harness steeds to the chariots, inhabitants of Lachish; You are the beginning of sin for daughter Zion, For in you were found the crimes of Israel.
text.DRB: Tumult of the chariot of astonishment to the inhabitant of Lachis: it is the beginning of sin to the daughter of Sion, because in thee are found the wickednesses of Israel.

text.NRSV: Therefore you shall give parting gifts to Moresheth-gath; the houses of Achzib shall be a deception to the kings of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, shalt thou give a dismission, against Moresheth-gath,—The houses of Achzib, served for a deception to the kings of Israel.
text.KJV: Therefore shalt thou give presents to Moresheth–gath: the houses of Achzib shall be a lie to the kings of Israel.
text.NABRE: Therefore you must give back the dowry to Moresheth-gath; The houses of Achzib are a dry stream bed to the kings of Israel.
text.DRB: Therefore shall he give spoilers upon the inheritance of Geth: the houses of lying into deception to the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: I will again bring a conqueror upon you, inhabitants of Mareshah; the glory of Israel shall come to Adullam.
text.Rotherham: The time shall yet be when, the heir, I will bring unto thee, O inhabitress of Mareshah,—as far as Adullam, shall enter the glory of Israel.
text.KJV: Yet will I bring an heir unto thee, O inhabitant of Mareshah: he shall come unto Adullam the glory of Israel.
text.NABRE: Again I will bring the conqueror to you, inhabitants of Mareshah; The glory of Israel shall come even to Adullam.
text.DRB: As yet will I bring an heir to thee which dwellest in Maresa: even to Odolla shall the glory of Israel come.

text.NRSV: Make yourselves bald and cut off your hair, for your pampered children; make yourselves as bald as the eagle, for they have gone from you into exile.
text.Rotherham: Make thee bald, and cut off thy hair, for the children of thy pleasures,—enlarge thy baldness, like a vulture, for they are exiled from thee.
text.KJV: Make thee bald, and poll thee for thy delicate children; enlarge thy baldness as the eagle; for they are gone into captivity from thee.
text.NABRE: Make yourself bald, cut off your hair, for the children whom you cherish; Make yourself bald as a vulture, for they are taken from you into exile.
text.DRB: Be bald, and be polled for the children of thy delicacies: enlarge thy baldness as an eagle: because they are led captives out of thee.

text.NRSV: Alas for those who devise wickedness and evil deeds on their beds! When the morning dawns, they perform it, because it is in their power.
text.Rotherham: Alas for them who devise iniquity and work wickedness upon their beds,—in the light of the morning, they will execute it, for it is in the power of their hand.
text.KJV: Woe to them that devise iniquity, and work evil upon their beds! when the morning is light, they practise it, because it is in the power of their hand.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 Ah! you plotters of iniquity, who work out evil on your beds! In the morning light you carry it out for it lies within your power.
text.DRB: Woe to you which think that is unprofitable, and work evil in your beds: in the morning light they do it, because their hand is against God.

text.NRSV: They covet fields, and seize them; houses, and take them away; they oppress householder and house, people and their inheritance.
text.Rotherham: Thus do they covet fields and seize them, and houses and take them away,—and so they oppress the master and his household, the man and his inheritance.
text.KJV: And they covet fields, and take them by violence; and houses, and take them away: so they oppress a man and his house, even a man and his heritage.
text.NABRE: You covet fields, and seize them; houses, and take them; You cheat owners of their houses, people of their inheritance.
text.DRB: And they have coveted fields, and violently taken, and houses forcibly taken away: and oppressed the man and his house, the man and his inheritance.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord: Now, I am devising against this family an evil from which you cannot remove your necks; and you shall not walk haughtily, for it will be an evil time.
text.Rotherham: Therefore,—Thus, saith Yahweh, Behold me! devising, against this family, a calamity,—from which ye shall not remove your neck, neither shall ye walk loftily, for, a time of calamity, shall it be.
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord; Behold, against this family do I devise an evil, from which ye shall not remove your necks; neither shall ye go haughtily: for this time is evil.
text.NABRE: Therefore thus says the Lord: Look, I am planning against this family an evil from which you cannot free your necks; Nor shall you walk with head held high, for it will be an evil time.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord: Behold I purpose evil upon this family: whence you shall not take away your necks, and you shall not walk proud, because it is a very evil time.

text.NRSV: On that day they shall take up a taunt-song against you, and utter a doleful lamentation, and say, “We are utterly ruined; the inheritance of my people is measured out; how it is removed from me! Among our captors our fields are divided.”
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall one take up against you a by-word, and lament a lamentable lamentation, saying—we are made, utterly desolate, the portion of my people, he passeth to others,—How doth he set me aside! To an apostate, our fields, doth he apportion.
text.KJV: In that day shall one take up a parable against you, and lament with a doleful lamentation, and say, We be utterly spoiled: he hath changed the portion of my people: how hath he removed it from me! turning away he hath divided our fields.
text.NABRE: On that day you shall be mocked, and there will be bitter lament: “Our ruin is complete, our fields are divided among our captors, The fields of my people are measured out, and no one can get them back!”
text.DRB: In that day a parable shall be taken up upon you, and a song shall be sung with sweetness, of them that say: With depopulation we are wasted: part of my people is changed: how shall he depart from me, whereas he returneth, that will divide our regions?

text.NRSV: Therefore you will have no one to cast the line by lot in the assembly of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, shalt thou have none to throw a measuring-line by lot,—in the convocation of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Therefore thou shalt have none that shall cast a cord by lot in the congregation of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Thus you shall have no one in the assembly of the Lord to allot to you a share of land.
text.DRB: For this cause thou shalt have none casting the cord of lot in the assembly of our Lord.

text.NRSV: “Do not preach”—thus they preach— “one should not preach of such things; disgrace will not overtake us.”
text.Rotherham: Do not sputter—So they sputter! They must not sputter as to these things, Must he not put away reproaches?
text.KJV: Prophesy ye not, say they to them that prophesy: they shall not prophesy to them, that they shall not take shame.
text.NABRE: “Do not preach,” they preach, “no one should preach of these things! Shame will not overtake us.”
text.DRB: Speak ye not speaking: It shall not drop upon these, confusion shall not apprehend them.

text.NRSV: Should this be said, O house of Jacob? Is the Lord’s patience exhausted? Are these his deeds? Do not my words do good to one who walks uprightly?
text.Rotherham: O thou who art said to be the house of Jacob, Is the spirit of Yahweh, impatient? Or are, these, his doings? Are not, his words, pleasant to him who is upright in his walk?
text.KJV: O thou that art named the house of Jacob, is the spirit of the Lord straitened? are these his doings? do not my words do good to him that walketh uprightly?
text.NABRE: How can it be said, house of Jacob, “Is the Lord short of patience; are these the Lord’s deeds?” Do not my words promise good to the one who walks in justice?
text.DRB: The house of Jacob saith: Why is the spirit of our Lord abridged, or are his cogitations such? Are not my words good with him that walketh rightly?

text.NRSV: But you rise up against my people as an enemy; you strip the robe from the peaceful, from those who pass by trustingly with no thought of war.
text.Rotherham: But, against my people, as an enemy, he setteth himself, from off the robe, they tear away, the cloak,—from such as are passing by with confidence, as men averse from war.
text.KJV: Even of late my people is risen up as an enemy: ye pull off the robe with the garment from them that pass by securely as men averse from war.
text.NABRE: But you rise up against my people as an enemy: you have stripped off the garment from the peaceful, From those who go their way in confidence, as though it were spoils of war.
text.DRB: And on the contrary my people is risen up as an adversary: from above the coat you have taken away the cloak: & them that passed simply, you turned into battle.

text.NRSV: The women of my people you drive out from their pleasant houses; from their young children you take away my glory forever.
text.Rotherham: The wives of my people, ye do even drive out, each from the house of her darlings,—from over her children, ye do take away mine ornament, as long as life shall last.
text.KJV: The women of my people have ye cast out from their pleasant houses; from their children have ye taken away my glory for ever.
text.NABRE: The women of my people you drive out from their pleasant houses; From their children you take away forever the honor I gave them.
text.DRB: The women of my people you have cast out of the house of their delicacies: from their little ones you have taken my praise for ever.

text.NRSV: Arise and go; for this is no place to rest, because of uncleanness that destroys with a grievous destruction.
text.Rotherham: Arise ye and depart, for, this, is not the place of rest,—Because it is defiled, it shall make desolate with a desolation that is ruthless.
text.KJV: Arise ye, and depart; for this is not your rest: because it is polluted, it shall destroy you, even with a sore destruction.
text.NABRE: “Get up! Leave, this is no place to rest”; Because of uncleanness that destroys with terrible destruction.
text.DRB: Arise, and go, because you have no rest here. For the uncleanness thereof it shall be corrupted with a sore putrefaction.

text.NRSV: If someone were to go about uttering empty falsehoods, saying, “I will preach to you of wine and strong drink,” such a one would be the preacher for this people!
text.Rotherham: If there be a man, who goeth after wind, and, falsehood, hath woven, [saying] —I will discourse to thee, concerning wine and strong drink, then shall he become a fountain of discourse unto this people.
text.KJV: If a man walking in the spirit and falsehood do lie, saying, I will prophesy unto thee of wine and of strong drink; he shall even be the prophet of this people.
text.NABRE: If one possessed of a lying spirit speaks deceitfully, saying, “I will preach to you wine and strong drink,” that one would be the preacher for this people.
text.DRB: Would God I were not a man having the spirit, and that I did rather speak a lie: I will distill to thee into wine, and into drunkenness: and it shall be this people upon whom it is distilled.

text.NRSV: I will surely gather all of you, O Jacob, I will gather the survivors of Israel; I will set them together like sheep in a fold, like a flock in its pasture; it will resound with people.
text.Rotherham: I will, surely assemble, O Jacob, all of thee, I will, surely gather, the remnant of Israel, at once, will I make them like sheep in distress,—Like a flock in the midst of its pasture, shall they hum with men,
text.KJV: I will surely assemble, O Jacob, all of thee; I will surely gather the remnant of Israel; I will put them together as the sheep of Bozrah, as the flock in the midst of their fold: they shall make great noise by reason of the multitude of men.
text.NABRE: I will gather you, Jacob, each and every one, I will assemble all the remnant of Israel; I will group them like a flock in the fold, like a herd in its pasture; the noise of the people will resound.
text.DRB: Gathering I will gather thee wholly together o Jacob: I will bring together the remnant of Israel into one, I will put them together as a flock in the fold, as cattle in the midst of sheepcotes, they shall make a tumult by reason of the multitude of men.

text.NRSV: The one who breaks out will go up before them; they will break through and pass the gate, going out by it. Their king will pass on before them, the Lord at their head.
text.Rotherham: One making a breach, hath gone up, before them, they have broken in, and passed through, and, by the gate, have departed,—and their king, hath passed through, before them, with, Yahweh, at their head!
text.KJV: The breaker is come up before them: they have broken up, and have passed through the gate, and are gone out by it: and their king shall pass before them, and the Lord on the head of them.
text.NABRE: The one who makes a breach goes up before them; they make a breach and pass through the gate; Their king shall go through before them, the Lord at their head.
text.DRB: For he shall ascend opening the way before them: they shall divide, and pass through the gate, and shall enter by it: and their king shall pass before them, and our Lord in the head of them.

text.NRSV: And I said: Listen, you heads of Jacob and rulers of the house of Israel! Should you not know justice?—
text.Rotherham: Then said I, Hear, I pray you, ye heads of Jacob, and ye judges of the house of Israel,—Is it not yours to know justice?
text.KJV: And I said, Hear, I pray you, O heads of Jacob, and ye princes of the house of Israel; Is it not for you to know judgment?
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 And I said: Hear, you leaders of Jacob, rulers of the house of Israel! Is it not your duty to know what is right,
text.DRB: And I said: Hear ye Princes of Jacob, and ye dukes of the house of Israel: Why, is it not your part to know judgement,

text.NRSV: you who hate the good and love the evil, who tear the skin off my people, and the flesh off their bones;
text.Rotherham: Ye haters of right, and lovers of wrong,—tearing away their skin from off them, and their flesh from off their bones;
text.KJV: Who hate the good, and love the evil; who pluck off their skin from off them, and their flesh from off their bones;
text.NABRE: you who hate what is good, and love evil? You who tear their skin from them, and their flesh from their bones;
text.DRB: which hate good, and love evil: which violently take away their skins from them, and their flesh from their bones?

text.NRSV: who eat the flesh of my people, flay their skin off them, break their bones in pieces, and chop them up like meat in a kettle, like flesh in a caldron.
text.Rotherham: Who indeed have eaten the flesh of my people, and, their skin from off them, have stript, and, their bones, have they broken in pieces,—and will spread them out, as flesh with a fork, and as flesh, in the midst of a pot.
text.KJV: Who also eat the flesh of my people, and flay their skin from off them; and they break their bones, and chop them in pieces, as for the pot, and as flesh within the caldron.
text.NABRE: Who eat the flesh of my people, flay their skin from them, and break their bones; Who chop them in pieces like flesh in a kettle, like meat in a pot.
text.DRB: Which have eaten the flesh of my people, and have flead their skin from them: and have broken, and cut their bones as in a kettle, and as it were flesh in the midst of a pot.

text.NRSV: Then they will cry to the Lord, but he will not answer them; he will hide his face from them at that time, because they have acted wickedly.
text.Rotherham: Then, shall they make outcry unto Yahweh, but he will not answer them,—that he may hide his face from them, at that time, even as they have made wicked their doings.
text.KJV: Then shall they cry unto the Lord, but he will not hear them: he will even hide his face from them at that time, as they have behaved themselves ill in their doings.
text.NABRE: When they cry to the Lord, he will not answer them; He will hide his face from them at that time, because of the evil they have done.
text.DRB: shall they cry to our Lord, and he will not hear them: and he will hide his face from them at that time, as they have done wickedly in their inventions.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord concerning the prophets who lead my people astray, who cry “Peace” when they have something to eat, but declare war against those who put nothing into their mouths.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh, concerning the prophets who are leading astray my people,—who bite with their teeth, and then cry—Prosper! and whoso holdeth not to their mouth, they hallow against him a war!
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord concerning the prophets that make my people err, that bite with their teeth, and cry, Peace; and he that putteth not into their mouths, they even prepare war against him.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord regarding the prophets: O you who lead my people astray, When your teeth have something to bite you announce peace, But proclaim war against the one who fails to put something in your mouth.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord upon the Prophets, that seduce my people: that bite with their teeth, and preach peace: and if a man give not something in their mouth, they sanctify battle upon him.

text.NRSV: Therefore it shall be night to you, without vision, and darkness to you, without revelation. The sun shall go down upon the prophets, and the day shall be black over them;
text.Rotherham: Therefore, shall it be night to you, for lack of vision, and darkness to you, for lack of divination,—and the sun, shall go in, over the prophets, and the day, shall be overcast because of them;
text.KJV: Therefore night shall be unto you, that ye shall not have a vision; and it shall be dark unto you, that ye shall not divine; and the sun shall go down over the prophets, and the day shall be dark over them.
text.NABRE: Therefore you shall have night, not vision, darkness, not divination; The sun shall go down upon the prophets, and the day shall be dark for them.
text.DRB: Therefore there shall be night to you for vision, and darkness to you for divination: and the sun shall go down upon the Prophets, and the day shall be darkened over them.

text.NRSV: the seers shall be disgraced, and the diviners put to shame; they shall all cover their lips, for there is no answer from God.
text.Rotherham: And the men of vision, shall turn pale, and the diviners, shall blush, and shall put a covering upon their lip, all of them,—because there is no answer of God.
text.KJV: Then shall the seers be ashamed, and the diviners confounded: yea, they shall all cover their lips; for there is no answer of God.
text.NABRE: Then the seers shall be put to shame, and the diviners confounded; They shall all cover their lips, because there is no answer from God.
text.DRB: And they shall be confounded that see visions, and the diviners shall be confounded: and all shall cover their faces, because there is no answer of God.

text.NRSV: But as for me, I am filled with power, with the spirit of the Lord, and with justice and might, to declare to Jacob his transgression and to Israel his sin.
text.Rotherham: But, in very deed, I, am full of vigour, with the spirit of Yahweh, and of justice and of valour,—to declare to Jacob, his transgression, and to Israel, his sin.
text.KJV: But truly I am full of power by the spirit of the Lord, and of judgment, and of might, to declare unto Jacob his transgression, and to Israel his sin.
text.NABRE: But as for me, I am filled with power, with the spirit of the Lord, with justice and with might; To declare to Jacob his crimes and to Israel his sins.
text.DRB: But yet I am replenished with the strength of the spirit of our Lord, with judgement, & power: to declare unto Jacob his wickedness, and to Israel his sin.

text.NRSV: Hear this, you heads of the house of Jacob and rulers of the house of Israel, who abhor justice and pervert all equity,
text.Rotherham: Hear this, I pray you, ye heads of the house of Jacob, and ye judges of the house of Israel,—who abhor justice, and, all right, do pervert:
text.KJV: Hear this, I pray you, ye heads of the house of Jacob, and princes of the house of Israel, that abhor judgment, and pervert all equity.
text.NABRE: Hear this, you leaders of the house of Jacob, you rulers of the house of Israel! You who abhor justice, and pervert all that is right;
text.DRB: Hear this ye princes of the house of Jacob, and ye judges of the house of Israel: which abhor judgement, and pervert all right things.

text.NRSV: who build Zion with blood and Jerusalem with wrong!
text.Rotherham: Building Zion, with deeds of blood,—and Jerusalem, with perversity.
text.KJV: They build up Zion with blood, and Jerusalem with iniquity.
text.NABRE: Who build up Zion with bloodshed, and Jerusalem with wickedness!
text.DRB: Which build Sion in blood, and Jerusalem in iniquity.

text.NRSV: Its heads give judgment for a bribe, its priests teach for a price, its prophets give oracles for money; yet they lean upon the Lord and say, “Surely the Lord is with us! No harm shall come upon us.”
text.Rotherham: Her heads, for a bribe, pronounce sentence, and, her priests, for a price, give direction, and, her prophets, for silver, divine,—yet, on Yahweh, they lean, saying, Is not, Yahweh, in our midst? there shall not come upon us, calamity.
text.KJV: The heads thereof judge for reward, and the priests thereof teach for hire, and the prophets thereof divine for money: yet will they lean upon the Lord, and say, Is not the Lord among us? none evil can come upon us.
text.NABRE: Its leaders render judgment for a bribe, the priests teach for pay, the prophets divine for money, While they rely on the Lord, saying, “Is not the Lord in the midst of us? No evil can come upon us!”
text.DRB: Her Princes judged for gifts, and her Priests taught for wages, and her Prophets divined for money: and they rested upon our Lord, saying: Why, is not our Lord in the midst of us? Evils shall not come upon us.

text.NRSV: Therefore because of you Zion shall be plowed as a field; Jerusalem shall become a heap of ruins, and the mountain of the house a wooded height.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, for your sake, Zion, as a field, shall be ploughed, and, Jerusalem, unto heaps of ruins, shall be turned,—and, the mountain of the house, shall [be] like mounds in a jungle.
text.KJV: Therefore shall Zion for your sake be plowed as a field, and Jerusalem shall become heaps, and the mountain of the house as the high places of the forest.
text.NABRE: Therefore, because of you, Zion shall be plowed like a field, and Jerusalem reduced to rubble, And the mount of the temple to a forest ridge.
text.DRB: For this, because of you, Sion shall be ploughed as a field, & Jerusalem be as an heap of stones, and the mount of the temple as the high places of forests.

text.NRSV: In days to come the mountain of the Lord’s house shall be established as the highest of the mountains, and shall be raised up above the hills. Peoples shall stream to it,
text.Rotherham: But it shall come to pass, in the afterpart of the days, that the mountain of the house of Yahweh shall be, set up, as the head of the mountains, and, exalted, shall it be, above the hills,—and peoples, shall stream thereunto;
text.KJV: But in the last days it shall come to pass, that the mountain of the house of the Lord shall be established in the top of the mountains, and it shall be exalted above the hills; and people shall flow unto it.
text.NABRE: II. Oracles of Salvation Chapter 4 In days to come the mount of the Lord ’s house Shall be established as the highest mountain; it shall be raised above the hills, And peoples shall stream to it:
text.DRB: And it shall be: In the later end of days there shall be the mount of the house of our Lord prepared in the top of mountains, and high above the hills: & peoples shall flow to it.

text.NRSV: and many nations shall come and say: “Come, let us go up to the mountain of the Lord, to the house of the God of Jacob; that he may teach us his ways and that we may walk in his paths.” For out of Zion shall go forth instruction, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Yea many nations, shall go, and say—come ye, and let us ascend unto the mountain of Yahweh, and unto the house of the God of Jacob, that he may teach us of his ways, and we may walk in his paths,—for, out of Zion, shall go forth a law, and, the word of Yahweh, out of Jerusalem;
text.KJV: And many nations shall come, and say, Come, and let us go up to the mountain of the Lord, and to the house of the God of Jacob; and he will teach us of his ways, and we will walk in his paths: for the law shall go forth of Zion, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: Many nations shall come, and say, “Come, let us climb the Lord ’s mountain, to the house of the God of Jacob, That he may instruct us in his ways, that we may walk in his paths.” For from Zion shall go forth instruction, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And many nations shall hasten, & shall say: Come, let us go up to the mountain of our Lord, & to the house of the God of Jacob: & he will teach us of his ways, & we shall go in his paths: because out of Sion shall the law go forth, & the word of our Lord out of Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: He shall judge between many peoples, and shall arbitrate between strong nations far away; they shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruning hooks; nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more;
text.Rotherham: And he will judge between many peoples, and be umpire to strong nations far and wide,—and they will beat their swords into ploughshares, and their spears into pruning-hooks, Nation—against nation—shall not lift up sword, neither shall they learn—any more—to make war.
text.KJV: And he shall judge among many people, and rebuke strong nations afar off; and they shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruninghooks: nation shall not lift up a sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.
text.NABRE: He shall judge between many peoples and set terms for strong and distant nations; They shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruning hooks; One nation shall not raise the sword against another, nor shall they train for war again.
text.DRB: And he shall judge between many peoples, and he shall rebuke strong nations unto a far off: and they shall cut their swords into coulters, and their spears into spades: nation shall not take sword against nation: and they shall no more learn to make battle.

text.NRSV: but they shall all sit under their own vines and under their own fig trees, and no one shall make them afraid; for the mouth of the Lord of hosts has spoken.
text.Rotherham: And they shall dwell—every man—under his own vine and under his own fig-tree, with none to make them afraid,—for, the mouth of Yahweh of hosts, hath spoken.
text.KJV: But they shall sit every man under his vine and under his fig tree; and none shall make them afraid: for the mouth of the Lord of hosts hath spoken it.
text.NABRE: They shall all sit under their own vines, under their own fig trees, undisturbed; for the Lord of hosts has spoken.
text.DRB: And every man shall sit under his vine, and under his fig-tree, and there shall be none to make them afraid: because the mouth of the Lord of hosts hath spoken it.

text.NRSV: For all the peoples walk, each in the name of its god, but we will walk in the name of the Lord our God forever and ever.
text.Rotherham: For, all the peoples, walk, every man in the name of his god,—we, therefore, will walk in the name of Yahweh our God, to times age-abiding and beyond.
text.KJV: For all people will walk every one in the name of his god, and we will walk in the name of the Lord our God for ever and ever.
text.NABRE: Though all the peoples walk, each in the name of its god, We will walk in the name of the Lord, our God, forever and ever.
text.DRB: Because all peoples will walk every one in the name of his god: but we shall walk in the name of the Lord our God for ever and ever.

text.NRSV: In that day, says the Lord, I will assemble the lame and gather those who have been driven away, and those whom I have afflicted;
text.Rotherham: In that day, Declareth Yahweh, will I take up her teat is lame, and, her that hath been an outcast, will I carry,—even whomsoever I have afflicted;
text.KJV: In that day, saith the Lord, will I assemble her that halteth, and I will gather her that is driven out, and her that I have afflicted;
text.NABRE: On that day—oracle of the Lord — I will gather the lame, And I will assemble the outcasts, and those whom I have afflicted.
text.DRB: In that day, saith our Lord, I will gather her that halteth: and her that I had cast out, I will gather up, and her whom I had afflicted.

text.NRSV: the lame I will make a remnant, and those who were cast off, a strong nation; and the Lord will reign over them in Mount Zion now and forevermore.
text.Rotherham: And will make of her that was lame a residue, and of her that was removed far away a strong nation,—and Yahweh, shall be king, over them, in Mount Zion, from henceforth, even unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: And I will make her that halted a remnant, and her that was cast far off a strong nation: and the Lord shall reign over them in mount Zion from henceforth, even for ever.
text.NABRE: I will make of the lame a remnant, and of the weak a strong nation; The Lord shall be king over them on Mount Zion, from now on and forever.
text.DRB: And I will make her that halted into a remnant: and her that had laboured into a mighty nation: and our Lord will reign over them in mount Sion, from this time now & for ever.

text.NRSV: And you, O tower of the flock, hill of daughter Zion, to you it shall come, the former dominion shall come, the sovereignty of daughter Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, O Migdal-eder, mound of the daughter of Zion, as far as thee, shall it come,—so shall arrive the chief dominion, the kingdom of the daughter of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And thou, O tower of the flock, the strong hold of the daughter of Zion, unto thee shall it come, even the first dominion; the kingdom shall come to the daughter of Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: And you, O tower of the flock, hill of daughter Zion! To you it shall come: the former dominion shall be restored, the reign of daughter Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And thou the tower of flock, cloudy of the daughter of Sion shall come to thee: and the first power shall come the kingdom to the daughter of Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Now why do you cry aloud? Is there no king in you? Has your counselor perished, that pangs have seized you like a woman in labor?
text.Rotherham: Meanwhile, wherefore shouldst thou cry out aloud? King, is there none within thee? or hath, thy counselor, perished? for labour, hath seized thee, as a woman in child-birth:—
text.KJV: Now why dost thou cry out aloud? is there no king in thee? is thy counsellor perished? for pangs have taken thee as a woman in travail.
text.NABRE: Now why do you cry out so? Are you without a king? Or has your adviser perished, That you are seized with pains like a woman in labor?
text.DRB: Now why art thou drawn together with pensiveness? Why, is there not a king to thee, or is thy counsellor perished, because sorrow hath apprehended thee as a woman in travail?

text.NRSV: Writhe and groan, O daughter Zion, like a woman in labor; for now you shall go forth from the city and dwell in the open country; you shall go to Babylon. There you shall be rescued, there the Lord will redeem you from the hands of your enemies.
text.Rotherham: Be in labour, and bear, O daughter of Zion, as a woman in child-birth,—for, meanwhile, shalt thou go forth out of the city, and dwell in the field, and shalt come as far as Babylon, there, shalt thou be delivered, there, will Yahweh, redeem thee, out of the grasp of thine enemies.
text.KJV: Be in pain, and labour to bring forth, O daughter of Zion, like a woman in travail: for now shalt thou go forth out of the city, and thou shalt dwell in the field, and thou shalt go even to Babylon; there shalt thou be delivered; there the Lord shall redeem thee from the hand of thine enemies.
text.NABRE: Writhe, go into labor, O daughter Zion, like a woman giving birth; For now you shall leave the city and camp in the fields; To Babylon you shall go, there you shall be rescued. There the Lord shall redeem you from the hand of your enemies.
text.DRB: Sorrow thou and labour o daughter of Sion, as a woman in travail: because now shalt thou go out of the city, & shall dwell in the country, and shalt come even to Babylon, there thou shalt be delivered: there our Lord will redeem thee out of the hand of thine enemies.

text.NRSV: Now many nations are assembled against you, saying, “Let her be profaned, and let our eyes gaze upon Zion.”
text.Rotherham: Meanwhile, therefore, shall be gathered against thee many nations,—who are saying—Let her be defiled, and let our eyes gaze upon Zion.
text.KJV: Now also many nations are gathered against thee, that say, Let her be defiled, and let our eye look upon Zion.
text.NABRE: And now many nations are gathered against you! They say, “Let her be profaned, let our eyes see Zion’s downfall!”
text.DRB: And now many nations are gathered together upon thee, which say: Let her be stoned: and let our eye look upon Sion.

text.NRSV: But they do not know the thoughts of the Lord; they do not understand his plan, that he has gathered them as sheaves to the threshing floor.
text.Rotherham: But, they, know not the purposes of Yahweh, neither have they discerned his counsel,—for he hath gathered them, as sheaves, to a threshing-floor.
text.KJV: But they know not the thoughts of the Lord, neither understand they his counsel: for he shall gather them as the sheaves into the floor.
text.NABRE: But they do not know the thoughts of the Lord, nor understand his plan: He has gathered them like sheaves to the threshing floor.
text.DRB: But they have not known the cogitations of our Lord, and have not understood his counsel: because he hath gathered them together as the hay of the floor.

text.NRSV: Arise and thresh, O daughter Zion, for I will make your horn iron and your hoofs bronze; you shall beat in pieces many peoples, and shall devote their gain to the Lord, their wealth to the Lord of the whole earth.
text.Rotherham: Arise and thresh, O daughter of Zion, for, thy horn, will I make to be iron, and, thy hoofs, will I make to he bronze, so shalt thou beat in pieces many peoples,—and shalt devote to Yahweh their unrighteous gain, and their substance, to the Lord of all the earth.
text.KJV: Arise and thresh, O daughter of Zion: for I will make thine horn iron, and I will make thy hoofs brass: and thou shalt beat in pieces many people: and I will consecrate their gain unto the Lord, and their substance unto the Lord of the whole earth.
text.NABRE: Arise and thresh, O daughter Zion; your horn I will make iron And your hoofs I will make bronze, that you may crush many peoples; You shall devote their spoils to the Lord, their riches to the Lord of the whole earth.
text.DRB: Arise, and thresh o daughter of Sion: because I will make thy horn of iron, and thy hoofs I will make of brass: and thou shalt break in pieces many peoples, and shalt kill the spoils of them to our Lord, and their strength to the Lord of the whole earth.

text.NRSV: Now you are walled around with a wall; siege is laid against us; with a rod they strike the ruler of Israel upon the cheek.
text.Rotherham: Meanwhile, shalt thou gather together in troops, thou daughter of a troop, siege, hath he laid against us,—with a sceptre, will they smite on the cheek, the judge of Israel!
text.KJV: Now gather thyself in troops, O daughter of troops: he hath laid siege against us: they shall smite the judge of Israel with a rod upon the cheek.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 But you, Bethlehem-Ephrathah least among the clans of Judah, From you shall come forth for me one who is to be ruler in Israel; Whose origin is from of old, from ancient times.
text.DRB: Now shalt thou be spoiled daughter of the spoiler: they have laid siege upon us, with a rod shall they strike the cheek of the judge of Israel.

text.NRSV: But you, O Bethlehem of Ephrathah, who are one of the little clans of Judah, from you shall come forth for me one who is to rule in Israel, whose origin is from of old, from ancient days.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, Bethlehem Ephrathah, though, little, to be among the thousands of Judah, out of thee, shall Mine come forth, to be ruler in Israel,—whose comings forth, have been from of old, from the days of age-past time.
text.KJV: But thou, Beth–lehem Ephratah, though thou be little among the thousands of Judah, yet out of thee shall he come forth unto me that is to be ruler in Israel; whose goings forth have been from of old, from everlasting.
text.NABRE: Therefore the Lord will give them up, until the time when she who is to give birth has borne, Then the rest of his kindred shall return to the children of Israel.
text.DRB: And Thou Bethlehem, Ephrata, art a little one in the thousands of Juda: out of thee shall come forth unto me he that shall be the dominatour in Israel: & his coming forth from the beginning, from the days of eternity.

text.NRSV: Therefore he shall give them up until the time when she who is in labor has brought forth; then the rest of his kindred shall return to the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, will he give them up, until the time when, one who is to bring forth, hath brought forth,—and, the remainder of his brethren, return unto the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: Therefore will he give them up, until the time that she which travaileth hath brought forth: then the remnant of his brethren shall return unto the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: He shall take his place as shepherd by the strength of the Lord, by the majestic name of the Lord, his God; And they shall dwell securely, for now his greatness shall reach to the ends of the earth:
text.DRB: Therefore shall he give them even till the time, wherein she that travaileth shall bring forth: & the remnant of his brethren shall be converted to the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: And he shall stand and feed his flock in the strength of the Lord, in the majesty of the name of the Lord his God. And they shall live secure, for now he shall be great to the ends of the earth;
text.Rotherham: Then shall he stand, and tend his flock in the strength of Yahweh, In the excellency of the name of Yahweh his God, have they endured,—For, now, shall he be great unto the ends of the earth;
text.KJV: And he shall stand and feed in the strength of the Lord, in the majesty of the name of the Lord his God; and they shall abide: for now shall he be great unto the ends of the earth.
text.NABRE: he shall be peace. If Assyria invades our country and treads upon our land, We shall raise against it seven shepherds, eight of royal standing;
text.DRB: And he shall stand, & feed in the strength of our Lord, in the height of the name of our Lord his God: & they shall be converted, because now shall he be magnified even to the ends of the earth.

text.NRSV: and he shall be the one of peace. If the Assyrian comes into our land and treads upon our soil, we will raise against him seven shepherds and eight installments of the people;
text.Rotherham: So shall this one be Prosperity. As for Assyria—when he shall enter our land, and when he shall tread down in our palaces, then will we raise up against him, seven shepherds, and eight princes of mankind.
text.KJV: And this man shall be the peace, when the Assyrian shall come into our land: and when he shall tread in our palaces, then shall we raise against him seven shepherds, and eight principal men.
text.NABRE: They shall tend the land of Assyria with the sword, and the land of Nimrod with the drawn sword; They will deliver us from Assyria, when it invades our land, when it treads upon our borders.
text.DRB: And this man shall be peace: when the Assyrian shall come into our land, and when he shall tread in our houses: & we will raise upon him seven Pastors, & eight principal men.

text.NRSV: they shall rule the land of Assyria with the sword, and the land of Nimrod with the drawn sword; they shall rescue us from the Assyrian if he comes into our land and treads within our border.
text.Rotherham: Then shall they shepherd the land of Assyria with the sword, and the land of Nimrod in the entrances thereof,—So shall he deliver from Assyria, when he shall enter our land, and when he shall tread down within our bounds.
text.KJV: And they shall waste the land of Assyria with the sword, and the land of Nimrod in the entrances thereof: thus shall he deliver us from the Assyrian, when he cometh into our land, and when he treadeth within our borders.
text.NABRE: The remnant of Jacob shall be in the midst of many peoples, Like dew coming from the Lord, like showers on the grass, Which wait for no one, delay for no human being.
text.DRB: And they shall feed the land of Assur in the sword, and the land of Nemrod in the spears thereof: & he shall deliver from Assur, when he shall come into our Land, & when he shall tread in our coasts.

text.NRSV: Then the remnant of Jacob, surrounded by many peoples, shall be like dew from the Lord, like showers on the grass, which do not depend upon people or wait for any mortal.
text.Rotherham: And, the remnant of Jacob, shall be, in the midst of many peoples, as dew from Yahweh, as myriad drops on plants,—which tarrieth not for man, nor waiteth for the sons of Adam.
text.KJV: And the remnant of Jacob shall be in the midst of many people as a dew from the Lord, as the showers upon the grass, that tarrieth not for man, nor waiteth for the sons of men.
text.NABRE: And the remnant of Jacob shall be among the nations, in the midst of many peoples, Like a lion among beasts of the forest, like a young lion among flocks of sheep; When it passes through it tramples; it tears and no one can rescue.
text.DRB: And the remnant of Jacob shall be in the midst of many peoples as dew from our Lord, & as drops upon the grass, which expecteth not man, & tarrieth not for the children of men.

text.NRSV: And the remnant of Jacob, among the nations, in the midst of many peoples, shall be like a lion among the animals of the forest, like a young lion among the flocks of sheep, which, when it goes through, treads down and tears in pieces, with no one to deliver.
text.Rotherham: And, the remnant of Jacob, shall be among the nations, in the midst of many peoples, as a lion among the beasts of the jungle, as a young lion among flocks of sheep,—who, if he passeth by, both treadeth down—and teareth in pieces, and none can deliver.
text.KJV: And the remnant of Jacob shall be among the Gentiles in the midst of many people as a lion among the beasts of the forest, as a young lion among the flocks of sheep: who, if he go through, both treadeth down, and teareth in pieces, and none can deliver.
text.NABRE: Your hand shall be lifted above your foes, and all your enemies shall be cut down.
text.DRB: And the remnant of Jacob shall be in the Gentiles in the midst of many peoples, as a lion among the beasts of the forests, and as a lion's whelp among the flocks of cattle: who when he hath passed, & trodden down, & taken, there is none to deliver.

text.NRSV: Your hand shall be lifted up over your adversaries, and all your enemies shall be cut off.
text.Rotherham: Let thy hand be uplifted against thine adversaries,—and, all thine enemies, shall be cut off.
text.KJV: Thine hand shall be lifted up upon thine adversaries, and all thine enemies shall be cut off.
text.NABRE: On that day—oracle of the Lord — I will destroy the horses from your midst and ruin your chariots;
text.DRB: Thy hand shall be exalted over thine enemies, & all thine enemies shall perish.

text.NRSV: In that day, says the Lord, I will cut off your horses from among you and will destroy your chariots;
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, in that day, Declareth Yahweh, That I will cut off thy horses out of thy midst,—and will destroy thy chariots;
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the Lord, that I will cut off thy horses out of the midst of thee, and I will destroy thy chariots:
text.NABRE: I will destroy the cities of your land and tear down all your fortresses.
text.DRB: And it shall be in that day, saith our Lord: I will take away thy horses out of the midst of thee, & will destroy thy chariots.

text.NRSV: and I will cut off the cities of your land and throw down all your strongholds;
text.Rotherham: And will cut off the cities of thy land,—and will pull down all thy fortresses;
text.KJV: And I will cut off the cities of thy land, and throw down all thy strong holds:
text.NABRE: I will destroy the sorcery you practice, and there shall no longer be soothsayers among you.
text.DRB: And I will destroy the cities of thy land, & will destroy all thy munitions, & I will take away sorceries out of thy hand, & there shall be no divinations in thee.

text.NRSV: and I will cut off sorceries from your hand, and you shall have no more soothsayers;
text.Rotherham: And will cut off incantations, out of thy hand,—and, users of hidden arts, shalt thou not have;
text.KJV: And I will cut off witchcrafts out of thine hand; and thou shalt have no more soothsayers:
text.NABRE: I will destroy your carved figures and the sacred stones from your midst; And you shall no longer worship the works of your hands.
text.DRB: And I will make thy sculptiles to perish, and thy statues out of the midst of thee: and thou shalt no more adore the works of thy hands.

text.NRSV: and I will cut off your images and your pillars from among you; and you shall bow down no more to the work of your hands;
text.Rotherham: And I will cut off thine images and thy pillars out of thy midst,—and thou shalt not bow thyself down, any more, to the work of thine own hands;
text.KJV: Thy graven images also will I cut off, and thy standing images out of the midst of thee; and thou shalt no more worship the work of thine hands.
text.NABRE: I will tear out the asherahs from your midst, and destroy your cities.
text.DRB: And I will pluck up thy groves out of the midst of thee: & will destroy thy cities.

text.NRSV: and I will uproot your sacred poles from among you and destroy your towns.
text.Rotherham: And I will uproot thy Sacred Stems out of thy midst,—and will destroy thy cities:
text.KJV: And I will pluck up thy groves out of the midst of thee: so will I destroy thy cities.
text.NABRE: I will wreak vengeance in anger and wrath upon the nations that have not listened.
text.DRB: And I will do vengeance in fury and in indignation among all the nations, that have not heard.

text.NRSV: And in anger and wrath I will execute vengeance on the nations that did not obey.
text.Rotherham: Then will I execute, with anger and with indignation, vengeance upon the nations,—of which they have not heard.
text.KJV: And I will execute vengeance in anger and fury upon the heathen, such as they have not heard.

text.NRSV: Hear what the Lord says: Rise, plead your case before the mountains, and let the hills hear your voice.
text.Rotherham: Hear, I pray you, what, Yahweh, is saying,—arise thou, maintain thy controversy before the mountains, and let the hills hear thy voice:—
text.KJV: Hear ye now what the Lord saith; Arise, contend thou before the mountains, and let the hills hear thy voice.
text.NABRE: III. Announcement of Judgment Chapter 6 Hear, then, what the Lord says: Arise, plead your case before the mountains, and let the hills hear your voice!
text.DRB: Hear ye what our Lord speaketh: Arise, contend in judgement against the mountains, and let the hills hear thy voice.

text.NRSV: Hear, you mountains, the controversy of the Lord, and you enduring foundations of the earth; for the Lord has a controversy with his people, and he will contend with Israel.
text.Rotherham: Hear, ye mountains, the controversy of Yahweh, and ye lasting rocks, the foundations of the earth,—for, a controversy, hath Yahweh, with his people, and, with Israel, will he dispute.
text.KJV: Hear ye, O mountains, the Lord’s controversy, and ye strong foundations of the earth: for the Lord hath a controversy with his people, and he will plead with Israel.
text.NABRE: Hear, O mountains, the Lord ’s case, pay attention, O foundations of the earth! For the Lord has a case against his people; he enters into trial with Israel.
text.DRB: Let the mountains hear the judgement of our Lord, and the strong foundations of the earth: because the judgement of our Lord is with his people, and with Israel he will be judged.

text.NRSV: “O my people, what have I done to you? In what have I wearied you? Answer me!”
text.Rotherham: O my people! what have I done to thee? and wherein have I wearied thee? Testify thou against me!
text.KJV: O my people, what have I done unto thee? and wherein have I wearied thee? testify against me.
text.NABRE: My people, what have I done to you? how have I wearied you? Answer me!
text.DRB: My people what have I done to thee, or what have I molested thee? Answer me.

text.NRSV: “For I brought you up from the land of Egypt, and redeemed you from the house of slavery; and I sent before you Moses, Aaron, and Miriam.”
text.Rotherham: For I brought thee up out of the land of Egypt, and, out of the house of slaves, I ransomed thee,—and I sent before thee, Moses, Aaron and Miriam.
text.KJV: For I brought thee up out of the land of Egypt, and redeemed thee out of the house of servants; and I sent before thee Moses, Aaron, and Miriam.
text.NABRE: I brought you up from the land of Egypt, from the place of slavery I ransomed you; And I sent before you Moses, Aaron, and Miriam.
text.DRB: Because I brought thee out of the Land of Ægypt, and delivered thee out of the house of them that served: and sent before thy face Moyses, and Aaron, and Mary?

text.NRSV: “O my people, remember now what King Balak of Moab devised, what Balaam son of Beor answered him, and what happened from Shittim to Gilgal, that you may know the saving acts of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: O my people! remember, I pray you, what Balak king of Moab, counseled, and what Balaam son of Beor, answered him,—from the Acacias as far as Gilgal, that ye may know the righteousness of Yahweh.
text.KJV: O my people, remember now what Balak king of Moab consulted, and what Balaam the son of Beor answered him from Shittim unto Gilgal; that ye may know the righteousness of the Lord.
text.NABRE: My people, remember what Moab’s King Balak planned, and how Balaam, the son of Beor, answered him. Recall the passage from Shittim to Gilgal, that you may know the just deeds of the Lord.
text.DRB: My people remember I pray thee, what Balach the king of Moab purposed: and what Balaam the son of Beor answered him: from Setim even to Galgal, that thou mightest know the justices of our Lord.

text.NRSV: “With what shall I come before the Lord, and bow myself before God on high? Shall I come before him with burnt offerings, with calves a year old?”
text.Rotherham: Wherewith, shall I come before Yahweh? bow myself to God on high? Shall I come before him with ascending-sacrifice? with calves of a year old?
text.KJV: Wherewith shall I come before the Lord, and bow myself before the high God? shall I come before him with burnt offerings, with calves of a year old?
text.NABRE: With what shall I come before the Lord, and bow before God most high? Shall I come before him with burnt offerings, with calves a year old?
text.DRB: What worthy thing shall I offer to our Lord? Shall I bow the knee to the high God? What shall I offer unto him holocausts, and calves of a year old?

text.NRSV: “Will the Lord be pleased with thousands of rams, with ten thousands of rivers of oil? Shall I give my firstborn for my transgression, the fruit of my body for the sin of my soul?”
text.Rotherham: Will Yahweh, be pleased, with thousands of rams? with myriads of torrents of oil? Shall I give my firstborn for my transgression? the fruit of my body for the sin of my soul?
text.KJV: Will the Lord be pleased with thousands of rams, or with ten thousands of rivers of oil? shall I give my firstborn for my transgression, the fruit of my body for the sin of my soul?
text.NABRE: Will the Lord be pleased with thousands of rams, with myriad streams of oil? Shall I give my firstborn for my crime, the fruit of my body for the sin of my soul?
text.DRB: Why, can our Lord be pacified with thousands of rams, or with many thousands of fat buckgoats? Why, shall I give my first born for my wickedness, the fruit of my womb for the sin of my soul?

text.NRSV: He has told you, O mortal, what is good; and what does the Lord require of you but to do justice, and to love kindness, and to walk humbly with your God?
text.Rotherham: He hath told thee, O son of earth, what is good,—what then is, Yahweh, seeking of thee, but, to do justice, to delight in lovingkindness, and humbly to walk with thy God?
text.KJV: He hath shewed thee, O man, what is good; and what doth the Lord require of thee, but to do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God?
text.NABRE: You have been told, O mortal, what is good, and what the Lord requires of you: Only to do justice and to love goodness, and to walk humbly with your God.
text.DRB: I will shew thee o man what is good, and what our Lord requireth of thee: Verily to do judgement, and to love mercy, and to walk solicitous with thy God.

text.NRSV: The voice of the Lord cries to the city (it is sound wisdom to fear your name): Hear, O tribe and assembly of the city!
text.Rotherham: The voice of Yahweh, to the city, crieth out, With safety for him who regardeth his name,—Hear ye the rod, and who hath appointed it.
text.KJV: The Lord’s voice crieth unto the city, and the man of wisdom shall see thy name: hear ye the rod, and who hath appointed it.
text.NABRE: The Lord cries aloud to the city (It is prudent to fear your name!): Hear, O tribe and city assembly,
text.DRB: The voice of our Lord cryeth to the city, and salvation shall be to them that fear thy name: hear ye o tribes, and who shall approve it?

text.NRSV: Can I forget the treasures of wickedness in the house of the wicked, and the scant measure that is accursed?
text.Rotherham: Even yet, are there, in the house of the lawless one, the treasures of lawlessness,—and the scant measure—accurst?
text.KJV: Are there yet the treasures of wickedness in the house of the wicked, and the scant measure that is abominable?
text.NABRE: Am I to bear criminal hoarding and the accursed short ephah?
text.DRB: As yet there is fire in the house of the impious, treasures of iniquity, and a lesser measure full of wrath.

text.NRSV: Can I tolerate wicked scales and a bag of deceitful weights?
text.Rotherham: Shall I be pure with lawless balances? or with bag of deceitful weights?
text.KJV: Shall I count them pure with the wicked balances, and with the bag of deceitful weights?
text.NABRE: Shall I acquit crooked scales, bags of false weights?
text.DRB: Why, shall I justify an impious balance, and the deceitful weights of the bag?

text.NRSV: Your wealthy are full of violence; your inhabitants speak lies, with tongues of deceit in their mouths.
text.Rotherham: For, her rich men, are full of violence, and, her inhabitants, have spoken falsehood,—and, their tongue, is deceitful in their mouth.
text.KJV: For the rich men thereof are full of violence, and the inhabitants thereof have spoken lies, and their tongue is deceitful in their mouth.
text.NABRE: You whose wealthy are full of violence, whose inhabitants speak falsehood with deceitful tongues in their mouths!
text.DRB: By which her richmen were replenished with iniquity, and the inhabitants therein spake lies, and their tongue was fraudulent in their mouth.

text.NRSV: Therefore I have begun to strike you down, making you desolate because of your sins.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, I, have made thee sick with smiting thee,—laying thee waste because of thy sins.
text.KJV: Therefore also will I make thee sick in smiting thee, in making thee desolate because of thy sins.
text.NABRE: I have begun to strike you with devastation because of your sins.
text.DRB: And I therefore began to strike thee with perdition for thy sins.

text.NRSV: You shall eat, but not be satisfied, and there shall be a gnawing hunger within you; you shall put away, but not save, and what you save, I will hand over to the sword.
text.Rotherham: Thou, shalt eat—and not be satisfied, but be shrunk with hunger within thee,—Though thou remove, yet shalt thou not set in safety, and, what thou dost set in safety, to the sword, will I deliver.
text.KJV: Thou shalt eat, but not be satisfied; and thy casting down shall be in the midst of thee; and thou shalt take hold, but shalt not deliver; and that which thou deliverest will I give up to the sword.
text.NABRE: You shall eat, without being satisfied, food that will leave you empty; What you acquire, you cannot save; what you do save, I will deliver up to the sword.
text.DRB: Thou shalt eat, and shalt not be filled: and thy humiliation in the midst of thee: and thou shalt apprehend, and shalt not save: & whom thou shalt save, I will give unto the sword.

text.NRSV: You shall sow, but not reap; you shall tread olives, but not anoint yourselves with oil; you shall tread grapes, but not drink wine.
text.Rotherham: Thou, shalt sow, but shalt not reap,—thou, shalt tread the olive, but shalt not anoint thee with oil, also the grape, but shalt not drink the wine.
text.KJV: Thou shalt sow, but thou shalt not reap; thou shalt tread the olives, but thou shalt not anoint thee with oil; and sweet wine, but shalt not drink wine.
text.NABRE: You shall sow, yet not reap, tread out the olive, yet pour no oil, crush the grapes, yet drink no wine.
text.DRB: Thou shalt sow, and shalt not reap: thou shalt tread the olive, and shalt not be anointed with the oil: and press muste, and shalt not drink the wine.

text.NRSV: For you have kept the statutes of Omri and all the works of the house of Ahab, and you have followed their counsels. Therefore I will make you a desolation, and your inhabitants an object of hissing; so you shall bear the scorn of my people.
text.Rotherham: For strictly observed are the statutes of Omri, and every doing of the house of Ahab, and ye have walked in their counsels,—to the end I may give thee up to desolation, and her inhabitants to hissing, that, the reproach of peoples, ye may bear.
text.KJV: For the statutes of Omri are kept, and all the works of the house of Ahab, and ye walk in their counsels; that I should make thee a desolation, and the inhabitants thereof an hissing: therefore ye shall bear the reproach of my people.
text.NABRE: You have kept the decrees of Omri, and all the works of the house of Ahab, and you have walked in their counsels; Therefore I will deliver you up to ruin, and your citizens to derision; and you shall bear the reproach of the nations.
text.DRB: And thou hast kept the precepts of Amri, and all the work of the house of Achab: & thou hast walked in their wills, that I might give thee into perdition, and the inhabitants therein into hissing, and the reproach of my people you shall bear.

text.NRSV: Woe is me! For I have become like one who, after the summer fruit has been gathered, after the vintage has been gleaned, finds no cluster to eat; there is no first-ripe fig for which I hunger.
text.Rotherham: Alas for me! for I am become as gatherings of summer fruit, as gleaning-grapes in harvest, there is no cluster to eat, the first ripe fruit, my soul, craved.
text.KJV: Woe is me! for I am as when they have gathered the summer fruits, as the grapegleanings of the vintage: there is no cluster to eat: my soul desired the firstripe fruit.
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 Woe is me! I am like the one who gathers summer fruit, when the vines have been gleaned; There is no cluster to eat, no early fig that I crave.
text.DRB: Woe is me, because I am become as he that gathereth in autumn the clusters of vintage: there is no cluster to eat, timely ripe figs my soul hath desired.

text.NRSV: The faithful have disappeared from the land, and there is no one left who is upright; they all lie in wait for blood, and they hunt each other with nets.
text.Rotherham: Perished is the man of lovingkindness out of the earth, and, upright among men, is there none,—they all, for bloodshed, lie in wait. Every man—for his brother, do they hunt as for one devoted to destruction.
text.KJV: The good man is perished out of the earth: and there is none upright among men: they all lie in wait for blood; they hunt every man his brother with a net.
text.NABRE: The faithful have vanished from the earth, no mortal is just! They all lie in wait to shed blood, each one ensnares the other.
text.DRB: The holy is perished out of the earth, & there is none righteous among men: all lie in wait for blood, every one hunteth his brother to death.

text.NRSV: Their hands are skilled to do evil; the official and the judge ask for a bribe, and the powerful dictate their heart’s desire; thus they pervert justice.
text.Rotherham: Of wickedness with both hands to make sure, the ruler, doth make demand—and the judge—for a recompense,—and, as for the great man, he, is putting into words the desire of his soul, So have they woven the net!
text.KJV: That they may do evil with both hands earnestly, the prince asketh, and the judge asketh for a reward; and the great man, he uttereth his mischievous desire: so they wrap it up.
text.NABRE: Their hands succeed at evil; the prince makes demands, The judge is bought for a price, the powerful speak as they please.
text.DRB: The evil of their hands they call good: the prince requireth, & the judge is in rendering: & the great man hath spoken the desire of his soul, & they have troubled it.

text.NRSV: The best of them is like a brier, the most upright of them a thorn hedge. The day of their sentinels, of their punishment, has come; now their confusion is at hand.
text.Rotherham: The best of them, is as a sharp briar, and, the most upright, worse than a thorn hedge, The day of thy watchmen—of thy visitation, hath come, Now, shall be their confusion!
text.KJV: The best of them is as a brier: the most upright is sharper than a thorn hedge: the day of thy watchmen and thy visitation cometh; now shall be their perplexity.
text.NABRE: The best of them is like a brier, the most honest like a thorn hedge. The day announced by your sentinels! Your punishment has come; now is the time of your confusion.
text.DRB: He that is left among them, is as a brier, and he that is righteous, as the thorn of the hedge. The day of thy speculation, thy visitation cometh: now shall be the destruction of them.

text.NRSV: Put no trust in a neighbor, have no confidence in a friend; guard the doors of your mouth from her who lies in your embrace.
text.Rotherham: Do not trust in a friend, do not put confidence in an associate,—from her that lieth in thy bosom, keep thou the doors of thy mouth;
text.KJV: Trust ye not in a friend, put ye not confidence in a guide: keep the doors of thy mouth from her that lieth in thy bosom.
text.NABRE: Put no faith in a friend, do not trust a companion; With her who lies in your embrace watch what you say.
text.DRB: Believe not a friend, and trust not in prince: from her that sleepeth in thy bosom keep the closures of thy mouth.

text.NRSV: For the son treats the father with contempt, the daughter rises up against her mother, the daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law; your enemies are members of your own household.
text.Rotherham: For, the son, treateth as foolish, the father, and, the daughter, riseth up against, her mother, the daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law,—The foes of a man, are the men of his own house.
text.KJV: For the son dishonoureth the father, the daughter riseth up against her mother, the daughter in law against her mother in law; a man’s enemies are the men of his own house.
text.NABRE: For the son belittles his father, the daughter rises up against her mother, The daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law, and your enemies are members of your household.
text.DRB: Because the son doth contumely to the father, and the daughter riseth against her mother, the daughter in law against her mother in law: and a man's enemies they of his own household.

text.NRSV: But as for me, I will look to the Lord, I will wait for the God of my salvation; my God will hear me.
text.Rotherham: But, I, for Yahweh, will watch, I will wait for the God of my salvation,—My God, will hear me.
text.KJV: Therefore I will look unto the Lord; I will wait for the God of my salvation: my God will hear me.
text.NABRE: IV. Confidence in God’s Future But as for me, I will look to the Lord, I will wait for God my savior; my God will hear me!
text.DRB: But I will look towards our Lord, I will expect God my saviour: my God will hear me.

text.NRSV: Do not rejoice over me, O my enemy; when I fall, I shall rise; when I sit in darkness, the Lord will be a light to me.
text.Rotherham: Do not rejoice, O mine enemy, against me, Though I fall, I shall rise again! Though I sit in darkness, Yahweh, is a light to me.
text.KJV: Rejoice not against me, O mine enemy: when I fall, I shall arise; when I sit in darkness, the Lord shall be a light unto me.
text.NABRE: Do not rejoice over me, my enemy! though I have fallen, I will arise; though I sit in darkness, the Lord is my light.
text.DRB: Rejoice not thou mine enemy over me, because I am fallen: I shall arise, when I have sit in darkness, our Lord is my light.

text.NRSV: I must bear the indignation of the Lord, because I have sinned against him, until he takes up my cause and executes judgment for me. He will bring me out to the light; I shall see his vindication.
text.Rotherham: The indignation of Yahweh, will I bear, for I have sinned against him,—until he take up my controversy, then will he do me justice, he will bring me forth to the light, I shall behold his righteousness.
text.KJV: I will bear the indignation of the Lord, because I have sinned against him, until he plead my cause, and execute judgment for me: he will bring me forth to the light, and I shall behold his righteousness.
text.NABRE: I will endure the wrath of the Lord because I have sinned against him, Until he pleads my case, and establishes my right. He will bring me forth to the light; I will see his righteousness.
text.DRB: I will bear the wrath of our Lord, because I have sinned to him: until he judge my judgement: he will bring me forth into the light, I shall see his justice.

text.NRSV: Then my enemy will see, and shame will cover her who said to me, “Where is the Lord your God?” My eyes will gloat over her; now she will be trodden down like the mire of the streets.
text.Rotherham: So shall she who had been mine enemy fear, and shame, shall cover her, who used to say unto me—Where is Yahweh thy God? Mine own eyes, shall look upon her, Now, shall she become one to he trodden down, like the mire of the lanes.
text.KJV: Then she that is mine enemy shall see it, and shame shall cover her which said unto me, Where is the Lord thy God? mine eyes shall behold her: now shall she be trodden down as the mire of the streets.
text.NABRE: When my enemy sees this, shame shall cover her: She who said to me, “Where is the Lord, your God?” My eyes shall see her downfall; now she will be trampled underfoot, like mud in the streets.
text.DRB: And mine enemy shall behold, and she shall be covered with confusion, which saith to me, where is the Lord thy God? Mine eyes shall look on her: now shall she be into conculcation as the mire of the streets.

text.NRSV: A day for the building of your walls! In that day the boundary shall be far extended.
text.Rotherham: On the day for building thy walls,—on that day, far away shall be thy boundary:
text.KJV: In the day that thy walls are to be built, in that day shall the decree be far removed.
text.NABRE: It is the day for building your walls; on that day your boundaries shall be enlarged.
text.DRB: The day that thy walls may be builded upon: in that day shall the law be made far off.

text.NRSV: In that day they will come to you from Assyria and Egypt, and from Egypt to the River, from sea to sea and from mountain to mountain.
text.Rotherham: The very day, that, against thee, shall one come from Assyria, and [from] the cities of Egypt; yea from Egypt, even unto the River, and from sea to sea, and from mountain to mountain:
text.KJV: In that day also he shall come even to thee from Assyria, and from the fortified cities, and from the fortress even to the river, and from sea to sea, and from mountain to mountain.
text.NABRE: It is the day when those from Assyria to Egypt shall come to you, And from Tyre even to the River, from sea to sea, and from mountain to mountain;
